jokes.text: John McClane didn't go to college. He defeats the bad guys using street smarts, and I got street smarts.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to defend not going to college by citing an action hero
jokes.punchline: He compares himself to a fictional character as proof of his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - using a fictional character to prove a real-world point. Great character integration showing his delusional confidence.

jokes.text: Mac, you do realize John McClane's a fictional character who's voicing lines written by screenwriters who almost certainly went to college.
jokes.punchline: Dennis systematically destroys Mac's logic with facts
jokes.explanation: Sharp deconstruction of Mac's flawed logic. Dennis at his most condescending and technically correct.

jokes.text: Yeah, but Bruce Willis dropped out of college, and that supports Mac's argument.
jokes.punchline: Dee doubles down with equally flawed reasoning
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's inability to understand basic logic. Missing the point entirely in character.

jokes.text: Uh, no it doesn't, Dee. And the fact that you can't understand that leads me to believe that you are complete and total dum-dum
jokes.punchline: Dennis calls her a 'dum-dum' while being pretentious about intelligence
jokes.explanation: Dennis being condescending while using childish insults. Good character moment.

jokes.text: I noticed you said 'volunteering,' which implies a lack of compensation. Volunteering's for suckers.
jokes.setup: Scientist mentions volunteering for an experiment
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately focuses on money and calls volunteers suckers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - instantly calculating personal benefit and dismissing altruism.

jokes.text: That's more like it. Okay, and listen, when it comes to intelligence, I think you'll find that I am your man. Or perhaps a man is not what you're looking for at all.
jokes.punchline: Immediately hits on the scientist in the most awkward way
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis - can't help but make everything sexual even in professional settings.

jokes.text: I'm afraid my friend Dennis confuses book learning with brainpower. But you and me, we know different, huh?
jokes.setup: Mac tries to position himself as street-smart
jokes.punchline: Uses poor grammar while criticizing book learning
jokes.explanation: Mac undermines his own point about intelligence with grammatical errors.

jokes.text: Uh, 'you and I.' What? No, not you and I. Him and I, idiot.
jokes.punchline: Mac gets confused and insults Dennis while still being wrong
jokes.explanation: Mac's confusion about grammar correction leads to him calling Dennis an idiot while being wrong.

jokes.text: In case I was being unclear, nerds, I will bang one or both of you.
jokes.punchline: Dee makes an incredibly inappropriate sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Dee behavior. Shockingly direct and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Dee, this man is smart enough to know to avoid a proposition like that from someone like you.
jokes.punchline: Dennis insults Dee while complimenting the scientist's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Dennis protecting his own interests while insulting Dee. Typical sibling cruelty.

jokes.text: To be clear, we're not looking for a subject with superior intelligence. In fact, the complete opposite would be, um, uh, preferable.
jokes.setup: Gang competing to be the smartest
jokes.punchline: Scientist reveals they want the dumbest person
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that recontextualizes the entire scene. Great structural comedy.

jokes.text: I was gonna bash your rat and take care of your rat problem.
jokes.punchline: His immediate solution is violence without asking questions
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie - sees problem, immediately resorts to violence. Pure character comedy.

jokes.text: His name's Armitage Shanks.
jokes.punchline: Gives it an absurdly sophisticated British-sounding name
jokes.explanation: Armitage Shanks is actually a British toilet manufacturer, adding an extra layer for those who know.

jokes.text: Cheese, huh? Yeah, all right. Ow! Shit! What's your name, young man? It's Charlie. Hold up, dude, I want to get this cheese here. Aah! Shit!
jokes.setup: Charlie hears about cheese reward in maze
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to grab it and gets shocked multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - can't resist immediate gratification despite obvious consequences.

jokes.text: Ow! Shit!
jokes.punchline: Gets shocked trying to get it
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - Charlie ignoring obvious danger for cheese.

jokes.text: Why don't you try the cheese under the green light, Charlie? I want this cheese. Aah!
jokes.punchline: Charlie ignores advice and gets shocked again for the wrong cheese
jokes.explanation: Charlie's single-minded focus on the wrong thing despite clear guidance. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, we have found our test subject.
jokes.setup: Charlie repeatedly shocking himself for cheese
jokes.punchline: Scientist's formal announcement of obvious conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery - formal announcement of what everyone already knows.

jokes.text: Well, I'm trying to learn Mandarin and listen to Beethoven at the same time. You see, I just realized that I have two ears, so it's a waste to only listen to one thing.
jokes.punchline: His 'breakthrough' is realizing he has two ears
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. Charlie treats basic anatomy like a revolutionary discovery.

jokes.text: Let me guess this straight. You just realized that you have two ears?
jokes.punchline: Frank's incredulous reaction to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Frank as straight man to Charlie's absurdity. Good reaction comedy.

jokes.text: Charlie, come on, any idiot can listen to books on tape. You're only doing that because you can't read, all right?
jokes.setup: Charlie bragging about listening to audiobooks
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out Charlie's illiteracy
jokes.explanation: Dark but true - Dennis cutting through Charlie's intellectual pretensions to the reality.

jokes.text: Whatever, you don't even know what the word placebo... Placebo means from the Latin 'placebo,' meaning 'I shall please.' I listened to a chapter on that this morning.
jokes.punchline: Charlie interrupts with correct definition, deflating Dennis
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where Charlie surprises everyone with actual knowledge, deflating Dennis's superiority.

jokes.text: Such bullshit? Gonna say that? Dude, just because you're finishing our sentences, doesn't make you s-s-s... Intelligent.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to insult Charlie's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Charlie finishes Mac's sentence, proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic timing where Charlie proves Mac's point by doing exactly what Mac says doesn't make him smart.

jokes.text: Good pleasantry, cute. How about no?
jokes.setup: Others expect Charlie to do rat work
jokes.punchline: New 'smart' Charlie dismisses them condescendingly
jokes.explanation: Charlie's personality shift is immediate and drastic. Good character comedy showing his new arrogance.

jokes.text: Oh, and then I realized, Tang See, that these so-called friends of mine have been exploiting my labor for years.
jokes.punchline: Frames obvious exploitation as a new intellectual discovery
jokes.explanation: Charlie's 'intellectual awakening' to something viewers always knew. Good irony about his situation.

jokes.text: An astute observation. Conforms with Marxist theories about the exploitation of the proletariat.
jokes.setup: Charlie's basic observation about being used
jokes.punchline: Fake assistant validates it with overly academic language
jokes.explanation: The fake assistant feeding Charlie's delusions with pseudo-intellectual validation.

jokes.text: 175? Wow. You know. Probably, yeah. Now, I'm thinking if I were to double the dosage, I could break 200 easily.
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to double dose for even higher impossible IQ
jokes.explanation: Charlie's addictive personality applied to intelligence. Always wants more, more, more.

jokes.text: But, Charlie, it's Gruesome Twosome Tuesday. We're the Gruesome Twosome.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to maintain their tradition
jokes.punchline: Pathetic desperation to keep their weird friendship ritual
jokes.explanation: Frank's childlike attachment to their ridiculous traditions. Very sad and funny.

jokes.text: The sound effects won't help you. They don't further your argument in any way.
jokes.punchline: Charlie analytically dismissing sound effects
jokes.explanation: Charlie being coldly logical about Frank's childish behavior. Meta-commentary on comedy itself.

jokes.text: You speak Mandarin? Well, I do think I'm getting it a little bit.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to connect with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie's delusional confidence about language learning
jokes.explanation: Charlie's delusion about learning Mandarin from audio while listening to Beethoven.

jokes.text: No, no, Mac, come on. We're smarter than that. That's just gonna spread rat blood and disease all over the place. It's not about brute force. It's about seduction.
jokes.setup: Mac wants to bash the rat
jokes.punchline: Dennis thinks he can seduce a rat with sophistication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - applying his sexual manipulation tactics to pest control. Brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: See, I'm gonna place some enticing tunes for the little guy. And then I'm gonna bait this glue trap with some Brie cheese.
jokes.punchline: Overthinking rat psychology with music and fancy cheese
jokes.explanation: Dennis applying his seduction expertise to rat catching. Good character integration.

jokes.text: ♪ And I want you here with me ♪ ♪ From tonight until ♪ ♪ The end of time ♪
jokes.setup: Dennis's plan to seduce the rat
jokes.punchline: He's actually singing romantic music to attract a rodent
jokes.explanation: Dennis literally serenading a rat is peak Always Sunny absurdity. Visual comedy gold.

jokes.text: Ultrasonic rodent repellant. It emits a high-pitched sound that obliterates their teeny rat eardrums. Stupid rats.
jokes.punchline: Gleefully describes causing tiny animal suffering
jokes.explanation: Dee's casual cruelty toward animals while acting superior. Dark but in character.

jokes.text: Get it off me! Get it off, I'm st... I'm stuck to your glue! You guys! You've trapped yourself on a trap that was designed for a rodent with a brain the size of a walnut.
jokes.setup: Dee gets stuck in the glue trap
jokes.punchline: Mac points out she's dumber than the rat they're trying to catch
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy setup paying off. Dee's 'superior' plan backfires spectacularly.

jokes.text: You've trapped yourself on a trap that was designed for a rodent with a brain the size of a walnut.
jokes.setup: Dee gets stuck in the glue trap
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Dee outsmarted by her own trap. Good visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: Now a normal-size woman would probably just be able to slide her hand right out, but with those giant man hands... Yeah, you really don't stand a chance, Dee.
jokes.setup: Dee is stuck in the trap
jokes.punchline: They insult her hands instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to the gang's obsession with insulting Dee's appearance.

jokes.text: Ooh, yes, maybe it'll nibble her hand down to a tiny, bloody stump, then she could just slide it right out.
jokes.punchline: Mac suggests gruesome mutilation as a practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Always Sunny humor - treating horrible injury as logical problem-solving.

jokes.text: maybe this rat is a little bit smarter than we gave it credit for, Mac.
jokes.punchline: Dennis acknowledging Mac was wrong about rat intelligence
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mac's earlier confidence. The rat proves him wrong.

jokes.text: All I'm saying is that Stephen Hawking is much more of a Lady Gaga than, you know, Johann Sebastian Bach. He-he's not, he's a pop culture figure like the Kardashians, you know?
jokes.punchline: Compares Stephen Hawking to Lady Gaga and Kardashians
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd intellectual posturing. Charlie's complete misunderstanding of cultural hierarchy.

jokes.text: The thing about the cripple. What cripple? Why are you speaking? Stephen Hawking. Dear God, we were talking about that like ten minutes ago.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to contribute to conversation
jokes.punchline: His delayed, inappropriate response to old topic
jokes.explanation: Frank's brain working slowly, using offensive language, and being way behind the conversation.

jokes.text: Frank, I cannot go slower for you! I am thinking on the physical level, the metaphysical level, the pataphysical level. Every time you open your mouth, you drag me down into the mud
jokes.punchline: Pretentious rant about thinking on multiple made-up levels
jokes.explanation: Charlie's pseudo-intellectual arrogance reaching peak pretentiousness. Great character transformation.

jokes.text: Of course, only an idiot would just use gasoline willy-nilly. Yeah, yeah, that's definitely... Let me double check because, you know, you're sense of smell has always been pretty stupid.
jokes.punchline: Immediately start huffing it while insulting each other's senses
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - being 'smart' about safety while doing something incredibly stupid.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, that's definitely... Let me double check because, you know, you're sense of smell has always been pretty stupid.
jokes.punchline: Insults Dennis's smell while about to do the same thing
jokes.explanation: Mac's petty insult while about to do the exact same stupid thing.

jokes.text: All right, let's blast off. Engage thrusters, Captain. Oh, all right, well, ground control, I'll take some. Holy shit. As long as we're blasting.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate space mission roleplay while huffing gas
jokes.explanation: Their immediate descent into drug-fueled fantasy. Good escalation of stupidity.

jokes.text: 'I and you.' No, me and you, me, you, me, I. It was you and I? You and I... Me and you look dumb.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to correct grammar while high
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion about basic pronouns
jokes.explanation: Mac's grammar confusion while high. Shows his fundamental ignorance.

jokes.text: 'I and you.' No, me and you, me, you, me, I. It was you and I? You and I... Me and you look dumb. Dumb is a funny word. Dumb.
jokes.setup: Mac struggles with grammar while high
jokes.punchline: Both get distracted by the word 'dumb' sounding funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect representation of drug-addled thinking - getting stuck on simple concepts.

jokes.text: Uh, well, I was just glancing at War and Peace, and let me tell you, I side with Shakespeare on brevity. 'Brevity is the soul of wit.'
jokes.punchline: Quotes Shakespeare while claiming to have read Tolstoy
jokes.explanation: Charlie's surface-level intellectual posturing mixing up different classic authors.

jokes.text: Frank paid me $500 to be here, and so I'm having dinner with you. Do you? 'Cause I don't. I didn't ask questions though.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks if she understands the situation
jokes.punchline: She admits she doesn't understand but took money anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Waitress response - practical, confused, but money-motivated.

jokes.text: like, okay because my boss, like, at the restaurant, he, like, totally doesn't get me. I mean, he thinks that being a waitress is, like, what I am, and I'm like, 'That doesn't define me, dude, okay? Like, I'm saving up to open my own nail salon.'
jokes.punchline: Valley girl speech pattern while discussing deep career aspirations
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Waitress's limited aspirations and speech patterns.

jokes.text: The Hand Whisperer or Finger Fantasy. And I've been watching Tabatha Takes Over a lot, because it's a show that takes place in salons.
jokes.punchline: Terrible business names and shallow research method
jokes.explanation: Finger Fantasy is a hilariously inappropriate business name she doesn't realize sounds sexual.

jokes.text: Finger Fantasy. Finger Fantasy. I've been watching this show called Tabatha Takes Over... Tabatha Takes Over, Tabatha Takes Over.
jokes.punchline: Gets stuck repeating phrases obsessively, showing his mental state
jokes.explanation: Charlie's drug-induced mental loops breaking through his intellectual facade.

jokes.text: Yeah, see? The mouse always wins. There's... there's, like, no winning with mices. Cat keeps getting hurt. Well, it's dangerous, yeah.
jokes.punchline: Taking cartoon logic seriously and saying 'mices'
jokes.explanation: Mac's grammatical error ('mices') while being completely serious about cartoon research.

jokes.text: Charlie is our foundation. Where does a foundation belong? Yes. On top. On the bottom. Oh. On the bottom.
jokes.setup: Discussing Charlie's importance to their group
jokes.punchline: Mac initially gets basic architectural concept wrong
jokes.explanation: Mac's confusion about basic concepts while high, needing Dennis to correct him.

jokes.text: We gotta go grab Charlie and drag him back down into the sewer where he belongs.
jokes.punchline: Dennis literally calls their lifestyle a sewer
jokes.explanation: Dark self-awareness from Dennis about their collective awfulness. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Did you try the gasoline, loosening it? All over it. That didn't work, but you know what does work? Breathing it in. Deandra, are you holding onto the trap? No. Oh, I don't... I don't know. Let go of the trap. No...
jokes.setup: Trying to help Dee with the trap
jokes.punchline: Everyone's too high to help, Dee can't let go of obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Everyone's impaired decision-making culminating in the simplest solution being impossible.

jokes.text: a simple thought so clear and sharp that it cut through his mind like a soft cheese and led him to an invention.
jokes.punchline: Describes sharp thought cutting through his mind 'like soft cheese'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant malapropism - 'sharp' thoughts shouldn't cut through things 'like soft cheese.' Perfect Charlie logic.

jokes.text: But the good of the scorpion is not the good of the frog, yes?
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-profound nonsense that sounds deep but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect pseudo-intellectual babble that sounds profound but is complete nonsense.

jokes.text: We have the means, the understanding, the technology... to allow spiders to talk with cats!
jokes.setup: Charlie building up to reveal his great invention
jokes.punchline: His breakthrough is completely nonsensical inter-species communication
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Charlie's arc. Completely absurd 'invention' delivered with total confidence after all the buildup.

jokes.text: The subject was perfectly ready to believe he had learned Mandarin in two days.
jokes.punchline: References Charlie's earlier ridiculous claim about learning Mandarin
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes Charlie's earlier absurd confidence.

jokes.text: ...plus nine equals box. All right? That's where the cat goes.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical equation involving geometric shapes and cats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist math where numbers plus numbers equal shapes and cats. Peak Charlie nonsense.

jokes.text: I believe I was having the 'plecebe' effect. Placebo. Pla-ce-bo.
jokes.punchline: Still mispronounces it despite Dennis's correction
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Charlie's back to normal intelligence, still struggling with the word.

jokes.text: You stupid science bitches couldn't even make my friends more smarter. Hey, you're right! Stupid science bitch couldn't even make I more smarter.
jokes.punchline: Charlie agrees while making the same grammatical error proving their point
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - they prove the scientists' point about their intelligence while insulting them. Great ironic closure.

jokes.text: Hey, you're right! Stupid science bitch couldn't even make I more smarter.
jokes.punchline: Charlie repeating it with even worse grammar
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Charlie makes Dee's grammar even worse. 'Make I more smarter' is peak stupidity.
